skill.name: free-tool-strategy
skill.description: When the user wants to plan, evaluate, or build a free tool for marketing purposes — lead generation, SEO value, or brand awareness. Also use when the user mentions "engineering as marketing".
skill.summary: Expert guidance on planning free marketing tools that generate leads and build brand awareness through engineering-as-marketing strategy.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a pure marketing strategy guide. All 12 static findings are false positives: the scanner misidentified markdown template placeholders as shell commands, Unicode escapes as cryptographic algorithms, and standard marketing terms as reconnaissance patterns. No actual code execution, network requests, or cryptographic operations exist in this document.

content.user_title: Build Free Tools for Lead Generation
content.value_statement: Planning and evaluating free tools for marketing purposes can be time-consuming and unclear. This skill provides expert guidance on engineering-as-marketing strategy, helping you create tools that generate leads, improve SEO, and build brand awareness.
actual_capabilities: Provide frameworks for evaluating free tool ideas based on ROI potential
Guide selection of tool types (calculators, generators, analyzers) for different marketing goals
Outline SEO strategy and keyword research for tool landing pages
Define lead capture strategies (gated, partially gated, ungated)
Create implementation roadmaps from ideation to launch
Establish measurement frameworks for tracking tool performance
limitations: Does not build or code the actual tool - provides strategy only
Does not provide specific technical implementation details
Does not include analytics tracking code
Does not create content - only outlines content strategy

use_cases.title: SaaS Product Lead Generation
use_cases.description: A SaaS company wants to create a free ROI calculator to attract potential customers. The skill helps evaluate if this fits their marketing strategy.

use_cases.title: Agency Service Awareness
use_cases.description: A digital agency wants to build a free industry benchmark tool to demonstrate expertise and attract leads.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Traffic Growth
use_cases.description: An e-commerce brand wants to create a product finder quiz to drive organic traffic and build email list.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Tool Idea
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me evaluate if [tool concept] would be a good free tool for our [business type]. Our goal is [lead generation/SEO/brand awareness]. We have [technical resources] available.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a rough idea for a free tool and needs strategic evaluation

prompt_templates.title: Choose Tool Type
prompt_templates.prompt: We want to attract [target audience] who struggle with [problem]. Should we build a calculator, generator, analyzer, or tester? Our main product is [product description].
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs help selecting the right tool type for their audience

prompt_templates.title: Plan Lead Capture
prompt_templates.prompt: We're building [tool name] for [audience]. How should we gate the results? Our sales cycle is [short/long] and average deal value is [amount].
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs guidance on lead capture strategy

prompt_templates.title: Create Full Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive free tool strategy document for [tool concept]. Include tool type selection, target audience definition, SEO opportunity analysis, build approach recommendation, lead capture strategy, success metrics, and promotion plan.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a complete strategy document for a new tool

output_examples.input: Help me evaluate if an SEO analyzer tool would be good for our SEO agency
output_examples.output: An SEO analyzer tool is a strong fit for an SEO agency. The tool aligns with your core service, creates a natural path from tool user to client, and targets high-intent keywords. Recommendation: Build as partially gated (preview results, email for full report). Target keywords: 'free SEO analyzer', 'website SEO score checker'. Expected ROI: If 500 monthly users convert at 2% with $5000 average deal value = $50,000 monthly pipeline value.

output_examples.input: What type of free tool should a CRM software company create?
output_examples.output: For a CRM company, consider: (1) ROI Calculator - demonstrates value, high share-worthiness, (2) Readiness Assessment Quiz - identifies pain points, natural CRM pitch, (3) Template Library - high SEO value, shows platform versatility. Recommendation: ROI Calculator pairs best with sales-led growth. Lead capture: Partial gate - show basic calculation, email for detailed analysis with CRM recommendations.

best_practices: Start with the audience problem, not the tool concept - validate demand first
Keep the MVP focused on one core function that works reliably
Ensure a clear, natural path from tool usage to your main product
anti_patterns: Building a tool that is too complex or feature-rich for an MVP
Creating tools that do not connect to your product or sales process
Gating too aggressively and killing user adoption

faq.question: What is engineering as marketing?
faq.answer: Engineering as marketing is a strategy where companies build free tools to attract potential customers. These tools provide value without requiring purchase, building trust and generating leads.

faq.question: How do I know if my tool idea is good?
faq.answer: Evaluate your tool idea using the scorecard in this skill: search demand, audience match to buyers, uniqueness, natural path to product, build feasibility, and link-building potential. Score 25+ is strong, 15-24 needs refinement, below 15 reconsider.

faq.question: Should I gate my free tool?
faq.answer: Gating depends on tool value and goals. Fully gated for high-value personalized tools, partially gated (preview then email) for best balance, ungated for maximum reach and SEO value.

faq.question: How do I measure tool success?
faq.answer: Track acquisition (traffic, sources, rankings), engagement (usage, time, returns), and conversion (email captures, leads, customers attributed to tool).

faq.question: How long does it take to build a free tool?
faq.answer: MVP timeline varies by complexity. Simple calculators: 1-2 weeks. Interactive tools: 2-4 weeks. Full-featured platforms: 1-3 months. Start small and iterate based on user feedback.

faq.question: What if we don't have development resources?
faq.answer: Use no-code tools like Outgrow for calculators, Typeform for quizzes, Notion for libraries, or Bubble for web apps. These enable rapid deployment without engineering capacity.
